title: Offline map hazard annotation standard
summary: Use date, source, direction, status, confidence, and expiry time for every blocked road, water point, shelter, clinic, fire, flood, or landslide mark.
content: Use date, source, direction, status, confidence, and expiry time for every blocked road, water point, shelter, clinic, fire, flood, or landslide mark. Cross out obsolete information without erasing the history needed for review. Do not mark an unverified rumour as a confirmed route closure or safe point.
materials: Notebook or printed card. Context: “Offline map hazard annotation standard”.
Pencil. Context: “Offline map hazard annotation standard”.
Relevant labels, contacts, and official alerts — for «Offline map hazard annotation standard»
steps: Use date, source, direction, status, confidence, and expiry time for every blocked road, water point, shelter, clinic, fire, flood, or landslide mark.
Cross out obsolete information without erasing the history needed for review.
Assign one person to act and another to verify the result for Offline map hazard annotation standard.
Record time, source, action, result, unresolved risk, and next review for Offline map hazard annotation standard.
warnings: Do not mark an unverified rumour as a confirmed route closure or safe point.
Official instructions and trained responders override this offline card for Offline map hazard annotation standard.
common_mistakes: Acting before location, time, hazard, and responsible person are clear for Offline map hazard annotation standard.
Failing to update the plan when conditions change during Offline map hazard annotation standard.
alternatives: Use a paper note, SMS, radio message, or trusted messenger when the main system is unavailable for Offline map hazard annotation standard.
failure_signs: The decision source, responsible person, or next review cannot be identified for Offline map hazard annotation standard.
The action creates a new hazard or delays qualified help during Offline map hazard annotation standard.
when_not_to_use: Do not use this card to override an evacuation order, emergency responder, clinician, product label, or local law for Offline map hazard annotation standard.
short_term: For Offline map hazard annotation standard, stabilize the immediate risk, communicate the decision, and schedule the next check before attention moves elsewhere.
long_term: After Offline map hazard annotation standard, update supplies, contact lists, maps, training, and the written plan from what actually happened.
if_method_fails: Stop Offline map hazard annotation standard, isolate the unsafe result, communicate the failure, and use the safest official or professionally supported fallback available.
environment_notes: Adapt Offline map hazard annotation standard to Greek heat, wildfire smoke, earthquakes, islands, mountain roads, apartment stairs, power cuts, water interruptions, mobility limits, and official alerts.
sources: CDC — Water, sanitation and hygiene emergencies: https://www.cdc.gov/water-emergency/index.html
